Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Response To Interrogatories-First Set
Off Calendar Feb-11-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. VALERIE BANTER OF SCHNADER LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 364-6735 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JONATHAN C. CROSS OF THE HERBERT SMITH FREEHILLS, TEL. NO. (917) 542-7824 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JOE WOODRING OF THE COOLEY LLP, TEL. NO. (310) 883-6400 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. DAVID J. COOK, TEL. NO. 989-4730 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS;
CHERI WINTER, COURT REPORTER.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS JOSEPH ALIOTO and THE ALIOTO LAW FIRM, A SOLE PROPRIETORSHIP's Motion To Compel Further Response To Interrogatories-First Set - Motion granted as to Nos. 2, 9, 10, 15 (as modified), 16 (as modified), 26 (as modified), 27, and 28. Within 15 days of service of notice of entry of order, Plaintiff LFG National Capital, LLC shall serve verified further responses. Objections are overruled as to Interrogatories nos. 2, 9, 10, 15, 16, 27, and 28. The Court finds Interrogatories nos. 2, 9, 10, 27, and 28 are sufficiently definite to define the scope of the interrogatory. Interrogatories nos. 15 and 16 are sufficient, but to avoid any possible ambiguity, the interrogatories are modified to reference the Plaintiff as the recipient and to refer to all payments made by Defendants to Plaintiff. For Interrogatories no. 26, the objection is sustained as to the phrase, "anything else," and the Interrogatory is modified to strike that phrase; the balance of the interrogatory is sufficiently intelligible to merit a response by Plaintiff. Monetary sanctions of $ 2,500.00 are awarded in favor of Defendants Joseph M. Alioto and The Alioto Law Firm and against Plaintiff LFG National Capital, LLC to be paid within 30 days of service of notice of entry of order. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Cheri Winter

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-05-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS JOSEPH ALIOTO & THE ALIOTO LAW FIRM'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS Denied. Defendants' raises issues of fact that are not the proper subject of a motion to strike. See CCP 436. Further, Defendants have presented extrinsic evidence that is improper. (See Cook Decl., filed Jul. 5, 2013, and Cook Decl., filed Jul. 26, 2013.) Further, Defendants rely on records and evidence without having requested judicial notice. In any event, the existence of a document may be judicially noticeable, but "the truth of statements contained in the document and its proper interpretation are not subject to judicial notice if those matters are reasonably disputable." (Fremont Indem. Co. v. Fremont General Corp. (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 97, 113.) Defendants' submission of the bill of particulars for the first time in reply is improper and is therefore not considered by the Court. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Melanie Gheno CSR7489.
